text.title: Video Gen Service — Procedure
text.summary: Six steps: persistent queue → workers → ffmpeg → quota + cost cap → idempotency → ship + monitor.

step.name: persistent-queue
step.action: Configure Redis Streams or SQS as job queue. Add dead-letter queue for permanent failures. Configure consumer groups.

step.name: workers
step.action: Deploy N workers per provider. Each worker speaks to async-api client. Configure concurrency per provider (Runway 5, Luma 3).

step.name: ffmpeg-post-process
step.action: Declare PostProcessing block in job spec: concat, overlay, transcode, watermark. Workers apply after artefact download.
step.decision-gate: If ffmpeg invoked inline in HTTP handler — fix (fm-03).

step.name: quota-and-cost-cap
step.action: Pre-submit check: tenant.this_month + this_job ≤ tenant.quota AND service.this_month + this_job ≤ cap × 0.95. Else reject.
step.decision-gate: If cap check after submit — fix (r4-cost-cap-pre-submit).

step.name: idempotency
step.action: On submit, look up Idempotency-Key in store; if present within window, return existing job_id without billing.

step.name: ship-and-monitor
step.action: Roll out at 10% → 50% → 100%. Monitor: queue depth, worker latency, ffmpeg duration, cost / day, per-tenant share, 429/402 rates.
step.decision-gate: If queue depth grows monotonically — scale workers OR rate-limit submit; queue is saturated.
